title: Remove PROGRESS.md; track state only in features/
description: Delete PROGRESS.md entirely. Feature status was the only thing keeping it useful, and that status already lives in features/*.json. Establish a 'flip to done before merging' convention so the closed status rides into main as part of the PR's own diff — no follow-up bookkeeping commit. Update AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, docs/HARNESS.md, docs/SESSION.md, docs/ARCHITECTURE.md, README, init.sh, scripts/check-clean-state.sh, scripts/harness-ci-checks.sh accordingly.
verification: PROGRESS.md is gone from the repo root
AGENTS.md startup workflow points at `features/` + `gh pr list` instead of PROGRESS.md
scripts/harness-ci-checks.sh dropped step 1 (PROGRESS check), now 5 checks; step 5 (branch name) renumbered correspondingly
scripts/check-clean-state.sh check 4 now validates that the in-flight feature is flipped to done before merge
init.sh has 6 steps (was 7); no longer tails PROGRESS.md
DECISIONS.md has a 2026-05-20 entry explaining the removal
This feature's status is flipped to done in the same PR's last commit, with commitSha set to the prior implementation commit
notes: Iteration on the earlier 'trim PROGRESS.md' scope after user said 'just remove progress entirely.' The close-before-merge convention is the harness's answer to the recurring bookkeeping gap (ci-actions after PR #3, harness-features after PR #6, docs-template after PR #7 — same shape three times). Flipping the status in the PR's own last commit means the merge carries the closed state into main as part of its diff.
